nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Understand-Anything exclusion patterns scoped to Orbit Wars project sources, omitting venv, OMG/Cursor tooling, training outputs, and MCP server from graph analysis.
nodes.languageNotes: Mirrors .gitignore-style globs with explicit negation for forcing includes under default excludes.

nodes.name: fingerprints.json
nodes.summary: Incremental analysis fingerprint store mapping project file paths to content hashes, version, and git commit for change detection across rebuilds.

nodes.name: knowledge-graph.json
nodes.summary: Merged Understand-Anything knowledge graph artifact with nodes, edges, layers, and tour metadata produced by prior full-repo analysis runs.

nodes.name: meta.json
nodes.summary: Lightweight metadata recording last analysis timestamp, git commit, schema version, and analyzed file count for the Understand-Anything workspace.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Canonical agent guide for Orbit Wars covering repository layout, uv/Hydra commands, JAX/Python subsystems, testing expectations, and OMG/MCP workflow notes.

nodes.name: config.yaml
nodes.summary: Root Hydra composition listing default responsibility groups (model, task, reward, training, format, curriculum, opponents, telemetry, artifacts) plus run seed, device, output paths, and sweep directories.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Hydra defaults list and custom resolvers such as orbit_run_id and orbit_safe_rel for output layout.

nodes.name: default.yaml
nodes.summary: Default artifact pipeline settings for checkpoint cadence, async replay validation via Docker, optional job queues, and checkpoint retention pruning by reward metric.

nodes.name: default.yaml
nodes.summary: Minimal curriculum group with training disabled and an empty stage list, used as the Hydra default when no staged progression is needed.

nodes.name: latest_only.yaml
nodes.summary: Curriculum profile that disables staged progression while keeping the schema compatible with latest-opponent training runs.

nodes.name: self_play_staged.yaml
nodes.summary: Three-stage self-play curriculum promoting from random opponents through mixed exploiters to self-play pressure using win-rate gates and opponent family mixes.

nodes.name: 2p_16env.yaml
nodes.summary: 2-player-only format schedule with 16 parallel two-player envs and zero four-player rollouts.

nodes.name: 2p_32env.yaml
nodes.summary: 2-player-only format schedule with 32 parallel two-player envs, matching the root config default format group.

nodes.name: 4p_16env.yaml
nodes.summary: 4-player-only format schedule weighting four-player games and allocating 16 four-player envs.

nodes.name: 4p_32env.yaml
nodes.summary: 4-player-only format schedule with 32 four-player parallel environments and no two-player rollouts.

nodes.name: empty.yaml
nodes.summary: Placeholder format group with empty schedule, mix, rollout groups, and phases for experiments that supply format at runtime.

nodes.name: mix_2p_4p_16env.yaml
nodes.summary: Mixed 2p/4p format profile balancing player counts with 16-env rollout groups for each format.

nodes.name: mix_2p_4p_8env.yaml
nodes.summary: Mixed 2p/4p format profile using 8 parallel envs per rollout group for lighter throughput.

nodes.name: attention.yaml
nodes.summary: Attention-based policy architecture preset with hidden size and head count for observation normalization.

nodes.name: entity_transformer_1m.yaml
nodes.summary: ~1M-parameter entity transformer architecture preset for larger-capacity experiments.

nodes.name: entity_transformer_500k.yaml
nodes.summary: ~500k-parameter entity transformer preset balancing capacity and training cost.

nodes.name: entity_transformer_700k.yaml
nodes.summary: ~700k-parameter entity transformer preset between 500k and 1M variants.

nodes.name: gnn_pointer.yaml
nodes.summary: GNN pointer architecture with neighbor message passing, max move horizon, and observation normalization—default in root Hydra config.

nodes.name: mlp.yaml
nodes.summary: MLP baseline architecture preset sharing hidden-size and normalization fields with transformer variants.

nodes.name: latest_only.yaml
nodes.summary: Opponent profile weighting entirely toward the latest checkpoint with self-play disabled and snapshot pool unused.

nodes.name: noop_only.yaml
nodes.summary: Opponent profile that routes mixed opponent selection to noop agents for debugging and curriculum bootstrap scenarios.

nodes.name: self_play_curriculum.yaml
nodes.summary: Hydra opponent profile enabling self-play with mixed opponent weights, snapshot pool cadence, and curriculum-friendly self-play toggles.

nodes.name: early_terminal_only.yaml
nodes.summary: Reward profile zeroing dense shaping while enabling early-terminal reward shaping with binary win terminal mode.

nodes.name: terminal_only.yaml
nodes.summary: Reward profile disabling all dense and early-terminal shaping, leaving only scaled binary terminal win rewards.

nodes.name: default.yaml
nodes.summary: Default task topology: 2-player games with candidate count, ship buckets, fleet limits, and optional trajectory-shield settings.

nodes.name: default.yaml
nodes.summary: Default PPO training budget and optimizer hyperparameters including rollout chunking, plateau reseeding, and lean rollout metrics.

nodes.name: default.yaml
nodes.summary: Full telemetry metric groups for core progress, losses, timing, curriculum, and events with W&B project defaults.

nodes.name: throughput_only.yaml
nodes.summary: Reduced telemetry profile emphasizing timing and throughput metrics while disabling heavier diagnostic groups.

nodes.name: 2p_only_throughput.yaml
nodes.summary: W&B grid sweep maximizing samples_per_sec for 2-player-only rollout formats with env and minibatch scaling.
nodes.languageNotes: Hydra-style dotted overrides in parameters map to responsibility groups consumed by src.train.

nodes.name: 4p_only_throughput.yaml
nodes.summary: W&B grid sweep maximizing samples_per_sec for 4-player-only rollout formats with parallel env scaling.
nodes.languageNotes: Hydra-style dotted overrides in parameters map to responsibility groups consumed by src.train.

nodes.name: baseline_sentinels.yaml
nodes.summary: Post-baseline sentinel smoke grid across models and candidate counts with short update budgets.
nodes.languageNotes: Hydra-style dotted overrides in parameters map to responsibility groups consumed by src.train.

nodes.name: baseline_stage1_comfort.yaml
nodes.summary: Stage-1 baseline comfort and throughput upper-bound grid across models, formats, and rollout sizes.
nodes.languageNotes: Hydra-style dotted overrides in parameters map to responsibility groups consumed by src.train.

nodes.name: baseline_stage2_stability.yaml
nodes.summary: Stage-2 baseline seed-validation grid for a fixed Stage-1 finalist configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: Hydra-style dotted overrides in parameters map to responsibility groups consumed by src.train.

nodes.name: budget.yaml
nodes.summary: W&B sweep exploring training budget axes such as total updates and rollout steps.
nodes.languageNotes: Hydra-style dotted overrides in parameters map to responsibility groups consumed by src.train.

nodes.name: capacity.yaml
nodes.summary: W&B sweep comparing model capacity variants via hidden size and attention head grids.
nodes.languageNotes: Hydra-style dotted overrides in parameters map to responsibility groups consumed by src.train.

nodes.name: curriculum.yaml
nodes.summary: W&B sweep over curriculum stage schedules and opponent-family weight parameters.
nodes.languageNotes: Hydra-style dotted overrides in parameters map to responsibility groups consumed by src.train.

nodes.name: gnn_pointer_reward_search.yaml
nodes.summary: Exploratory W&B Bayesian/grid search for gnn_pointer reward shaping with run cap and validation metric.
nodes.languageNotes: Hydra-style dotted overrides in parameters map to responsibility groups consumed by src.train.

nodes.name: gnn_pointer_reward_validate.yaml
nodes.summary: Validation W&B sweep confirming top gnn_pointer reward configurations from the search phase.
nodes.languageNotes: Hydra-style dotted overrides in parameters map to responsibility groups consumed by src.train.

nodes.name: reward.yaml
nodes.summary: W&B grid sweep over reward shaping coefficients and terminal reward modes.
nodes.languageNotes: Hydra-style dotted overrides in parameters map to responsibility groups consumed by src.train.

nodes.name: task_complexity.yaml
nodes.summary: W&B sweep varying task complexity knobs like candidate count and ship bucket count.
nodes.languageNotes: Hydra-style dotted overrides in parameters map to responsibility groups consumed by src.train.

nodes.name: throughput.yaml
nodes.summary: General throughput W&B grid sweeping num_envs, rollout_steps, and minibatch sizes for attention baseline.
nodes.languageNotes: Hydra-style dotted overrides in parameters map to responsibility groups consumed by src.train.

nodes.name: baseline_sweep_results.md
nodes.summary: Recorded outcomes from the workstation baseline sweep: selected finalist, Stage-1 comfort screen, Stage-2 seed validation, and sentinel check summaries.

nodes.name: baseline_sweep.md
nodes.summary: Operational guide for the three-stage workstation baseline workflow, comfort gates, promotion rubric, and linked W&B sweep YAML files.

nodes.name: config_migration.md
nodes.summary: Short migration table mapping legacy flat YAML intents to Hydra responsibility-group override paths.

nodes.name: experiments.md
nodes.summary: Primary Hydra launch reference covering basic training commands, responsibility map, multirun sweeps, and W&B sweep invocation.

nodes.name: hydra_migration.md
nodes.summary: Hydra migration guide with old-to-new launch examples and notes on removed compatibility aliases.

nodes.name: ONBOARDING.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive onboarding guide covering architecture layers, key RL concepts, guided tour, file map, complexity hotspots, and verification matrix for new contributors.

nodes.name: Makefile
nodes.summary: Minimal build automation defining setup (uv sync) and test (pytest) targets for local development.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python project manifest declaring JAX/Flax/Hydra dependencies, uv dev groups, pytest paths, and Pyright settings for the RL codebase.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview with Hydra training entrypoint, responsibility-based config groups, sweep examples, and core development commands.

nodes.summary: CLI benchmark harness measuring JAX rollout and PPO update throughput across configured model and environment settings.

layers.name: Training Runtime
layers.description: JAX/Python RL stack: Hydra entrypoint, game simulation, feature encoding, PPO policies, opponents, curriculum, telemetry, and checkpoint artifact pipelines under src/.

layers.name: Hydra Configuration
layers.description: Responsibility-group YAML under conf/ composing models, tasks, rewards, training budgets, formats, opponents, curriculum stages, telemetry, artifacts, and W&B sweep grids.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: Pytest coverage validating Hydra config consolidation, JAX env/policy/PPO parity, features, curriculum, telemetry, artifacts, and submission packaging.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Contributor guides for Hydra experiments, baseline sweeps, onboarding architecture tours, and config migration under docs/.

layers.name: Scripts
layers.description: Operational CLIs for JAX throughput benchmarks, artifact workers, Kaggle Docker validation, attention log comparison, and IDE tooling sync.

layers.name: Project Root
layers.description: Repository entry surfaces: README and AGENTS agent guide, uv/pyproject manifest, Makefile targets, and Understand-Anything analysis metadata.

tour.title: Hydra Training Entry
tour.description: Start where README.md points newcomers: `uv run python -m src.train` is the canonical launch path. This thin Hydra CLI resolves overrides from `conf/` responsibility groups (model, task, reward, training, format, curriculum, opponents, telemetry, artifacts) into a typed `TrainConfig`, then hands execution to the JAX stack. Treat it as the map of how experiments are named and started—not where learning happens.
tour.languageLesson: Hydra composes YAML groups at runtime; CLI overrides like `model=attention training.total_updates=1000` map to nested config without editing defaults on disk.

tour.title: Config Schema & Runtime
tour.description: Building on the entrypoint, `src/config/schema.py` defines the dataclass contract every responsibility group must satisfy—player counts, rollout groups, PPO hyperparameters, and artifact cadence. `src/config/runtime.py` registers OmegaConf resolvers, composes Hydra output, and validates curriculum and telemetry settings before training starts. Together they are the bridge between `conf/*.yaml` files and the objects `src/jax/train.py` consumes.

tour.title: JAX Training Orchestrator
tour.description: After configuration is resolved, `src/jax/train.py` becomes the real control plane: it selects devices, builds vectorized environments, runs the PPO update loop, advances curriculum stages, logs metrics, and schedules checkpoint/artifact work. Follow this file to see how a Hydra run becomes thousands of batched JAX steps rather than reading game rules directly.

tour.title: Game Rules & Reference Env
tour.description: Orbit Wars mechanics live under `src/game/`: shared limits in `constants.py`, structured state in `types.py`, and a readable Python `env.py` that implements reset/step, opponent hooks, and reward shaping. The JAX trainer mirrors this semantics; parity tests treat this package as ground truth when batched code diverges.

tour.title: Observation Features
tour.description: Policies never see raw planets directly—`src/features/registry.py` fixes ordered feature slices and dimensions, `encoding.py` builds self/candidate/global tensors with history on the Python path, and `src/jax/features.py` reproduces the same layout for JIT rollouts. Changes here ripple through policy input shapes, checkpoint compatibility checks, and both Python and JAX tests.

tour.title: JAX Environment & PPO
tour.description: The accelerated loop pairs `src/jax/env.py`—batched stepping, combat, mixed 2p/4p formats—with `src/jax/ppo.py`, which collects rollouts, applies trajectory shields, and performs PPO updates. This is the heart of training throughput: environment transitions supply rewards and masks; PPO turns trajectories into gradient steps for the policy modules configured via Hydra `model=`.
tour.languageLesson: JAX `jit`/`vmap` batch independent envs on device; PPO reuses the same static shapes defined by task config (players, candidates, history frames) to avoid recompilation.

tour.title: Policy Architectures
tour.description: `src/jax/policy.py` implements Flax modules (MLP, attention, entity transformer, GNN pointer) with shared value heads and action masking hooks. Hydra `conf/model/*.yaml` selects architecture presets; this file shows how observations flow through encoders to action logits and value estimates used in `ppo.py`.

tour.title: Safe Launches & Opponents
tour.description: Gameplay safety and training diversity meet in `src/game/trajectory_shield.py`, which blocks illegal fleet launches during rollouts, while `src/opponents/pool.py` and `runtime.py` mix random, scripted, self-play, and snapshot opponents. `src/training/curriculum.py` promotes formats and opponent families using rolling win-rate gates tied to Hydra `curriculum=` and `opponents=` groups.

tour.title: Telemetry & Experiment Logging
tour.description: Training visibility is centralized in `src/telemetry/metric_registry.py`, which names metric groups toggled by `telemetry=` configs, and `logger.py`, which flattens rollout/PPO scalars for W&B or JSONL. These modules decouple `jax/train.py` from any one backend so throughput-only runs can drop heavy diagnostic groups without touching the learner.

tour.title: Checkpoints & Artifact Pipeline
tour.description: Long runs finish in `src/artifacts/`: `pipeline.py` queues async checkpoint and replay jobs, `replay.py` evaluates saved policies against configured opponents, and `checkpoint_compat.py` rejects stale checkpoints when feature schemas or config ownership change. `run_paths.py` (pulled in by the orchestrator) ties Hydra output directories to manifests—follow these when debugging missing checkpoints after a config migration.
